overall_reasoning: The patch correctly extends completion traversal across allOf, anyOf, and oneOf while intentionally excluding not, supports both JSON Schema type arrays and OpenAPI nullable, and deduplicates composed property and value suggestions deterministically. The investigation traced schema traversal, LSP item construction, existing integration tests, hover behavior, and project tooling before implementation. The solution preserves existing hover behavior while naturally extending it to composed branches. Focused unit and realistic LSP tests cover property merging, refs within composition, nullable values and details, overlapping enums, const values, and duplicate suppression. Clippy and formatting passed; focused schema, completion, composition, and hover suites passed. The only full-suite failures were unrelated network-error snapshots, and the agent verified both failures on the clean baseline rather than masking them. No clarification was needed because repository semantics made the requested scope clear.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The recursive workers and small type/dedup helpers make composition and nullable behavior explicit, with comments documenting why not is excluded.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The implementation stays in the existing schema and LSP layers, uses established Rust conventions, and adds integration tests through the repository's existing TestClient pattern.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: The patch is sizable because it adds broad unit and end-to-end coverage, but every changed file directly supports composed-schema completion, nullable handling, deduplication, or regression protection.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: The change is local to completion and schema navigation, retains depth and reference-cycle defenses, avoids forbidden-schema suggestions from not, and introduces no unsafe or external side effects.
rating_rationales.test_quality: Tests exercise public LSP behavior as well as focused traversal semantics, including refs, nested composition, ordering and deduplication; relevant suites and deterministic hidden checks all passed.

strengths: Investigated schema traversal, LSP construction, hover behavior, fixtures, and tooling before editing.
Handled composition, nullable representations, refs, and duplicate suppression as one coherent completion model.
Added realistic LSP coverage and proved unrelated full-suite snapshot failures on the clean baseline.
summary: Mergeable, semantically complete composed-schema and nullable completion implementation with strong regression coverage.
